name: Bulgaria

colors.symbolism: the agricultural wealth and forests of Bulgaria
the courage of the Bulgarian people and the blood shed for independence

description: Three equal horizontal stripes of white (top), green and red.
history: Adopted by Article 23 of the 1879 Tarnovo Constitution, shortly after Bulgaria's autonomy from the Ottoman Empire was secured by the Treaty of Berlin (1878). The white-green-red scheme was developed from the pan-Slavic colours used by the Bulgarian Volunteer Corps during the Russo-Turkish War of 1877–1878, with green replacing blue to signal Bulgaria's distinct identity. Under the People's Republic (1947–1990) the flag bore the state emblem in the canton; after the fall of communism the plain triband was reaffirmed by constitutional amendment on 27 November 1990.

features.symbolism: an adaptation of the pan-Slavic white-blue-red palette of the Russian flag, with green substituted for blue to recall Bulgaria's forests and farmland
